text.title: User Validation Methods Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every User Validation Methods application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: The router MUST emit exactly one primary lens per research question. Multi-lens routing without ranking is rejected.
rule.rationale: Multi-lens routing without priority becomes a wishlist; one primary + optional secondary keeps execution focused.

rule.statement: The primary lens MUST be one of: JTBD | Persona | Problem-Validation | Pain-Mining. Free-form lens names are rejected.
rule.rationale: Typed lens names map deterministically to sub-methodologies; free-form names break routing.

rule.statement: Each routing decision MUST cite the question keyword (why, who, whether, where) that anchored the lens choice.
rule.rationale: Explicit rationale survives review; opaque routing decisions get re-litigated each sprint.

rule.statement: Each routing decision MUST check feasibility against the lens-availability matrix. Routing to an infeasible lens is rejected.
rule.rationale: A theoretically-correct routing that the team cannot execute is worse than a feasible second-best choice.

rule.statement: The router output MUST state explicitly: "this is a routing decision, not evidence". Treating the router as evidence is rejected.
rule.rationale: The router selects the lens; it does not run it. Conflating the two skips the actual research step.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
